description: Schematic conceptual graph-schema and evaluation-setup figure.
notes: Non-numeric source notes only; the locked PNG is the visual ground truth.

description: The graph schema depicts herbs, compounds, and targets as distinct node classes with separate visual encodings.
notes: Defines the entities used in the evidence-stratified graph.

description: Green dashed herb-compound edges represent label-free herb context.
notes: Used for herb context without requiring held-out herb-target labels.

description: Blue dashed herb-target edges represent train-only herb-target supervision.
notes: The figure notes this supervision is not used for held-out herb encoding.

description: Orange compound-target edges represent evidence-stratified context connecting compounds to targets.
notes: Supports target scoring through shared graph context.

description: Blue solid target-target edges represent shared target context.
The relation table links each edge type to its graphical style and role in the graph schema.
The training graph panel shows train herbs connected to compounds and targets, with validation and test herbs excluded.
The held-out herb query panel shows an unseen herb encoded through allowed label-free herb-compound context and shared compound/target graph context.
notes: Represents the inductive query setup for held-out herbs.

description: Red dashed held-out herb-target links are marked as forbidden during scoring.
notes: Specifies that held-out herb-target labels are not available during query encoding or scoring.

description: The figure states that compounds and targets are shared frozen graph context.
notes: Clarifies the transductive context used while the held-out herb remains label-free.

description: The target scoring block ranks candidate targets for the held-out herb over the shared graph context.
notes: Evaluation labels are applied only after scoring.

description: The bottom callout states that evaluation labels are applied after scoring.
notes: Separates model scoring from later evaluation-label use.

description: The scope table distinguishes evaluated held-out herbs, shared compound and target context, unseen targets, and fully inductive all-graph learning.
notes: Clarifies what is evaluated, used as context, not evaluated, or not claimed.

description: The ranking schematic shows a held-out herb mapped to ranked candidate targets with recorded positives distinguished from unlabeled comparison candidates.
notes: Unrecorded pairs are explicitly not treated as confirmed negatives.

description: The bottom callout states the primary metric as macro herb-level AUPR over all candidate targets per held-out herb.
notes: Metric statement is part of the conceptual evaluation setup, not a generated results table.
